So I got him like all of the good, like renal food.
Yeah.
Yes.

Okay.
Okay.
And so just ran her to the vet.

Right.

A lot, but, like, not eating weight.

Okay.
But, yeah, I think drinking more than usual, probably.
But I just thought she was thirsty.
Yeah, yeah.
I don't know.

So I don't see them as often as I used to when they were living in Bosnia.

And he doesn't drink that much water.
So, yeah, I was, like, suspecting maybe diabetes, but his blood, like, his sugar level.
Was a little bit higher, but the vet said it was just stress because he hates being like in the car in a carrier.
He like freaks out.
So.
Yeah.
Yeah.
I think that's, that's what they were always like.
I think it's stress.

Yeah.
Yeah.
I don't know.

Yes, absolutely.
Yeah.
Yeah.

Yeah.
Yeah.
I don't know if I could stick her with a needle twice a day.

I think that would be horrible.
I think at some point she would start hiding from you.
Yeah.

Yeah.

I'm on all of

And then three kickoffs next week.
Yay.
Okay.
Yeah, we need another ME.
Another ME.

It's honestly a huge help.
Um, because it takes time, um, to, like, put together, like...
Initial content strategies, as you know, I'm sure.
Yeah, I'm learning quite a bit there.
Yeah, yeah.
Coming from a non-content background to being pushed into content strategy, I'm just like, I am hoping that these prompts that we use are somewhere close to right.
Yeah, yeah.
It's always like, I think the process that Jo put together is really good.
So she like gets you in a good, the one thing that I've been kind of noticing is sometimes the clusters can be too narrow.
So that's the one thing.
Hi.
Hi, I'm sorry, I'm late.
No worries.
How are you?
Great.

Cool.

Yeah.
Okay.

Yeah.

We have AI features and we have the most enterprise customers.
So I checked it down.

Basically it's like an article explaining why Ocutec is for small businesses, like a full article about it.

Sec here, but if you search for AppSec, it shows up 10 times.

I want to be aggressive, too, as I guess, whatever we can do.
I listed here, basically, what I want to do is, Sokseg is the best...

Nope.
It shows us like doing it as AI features, and then one thing that I've noticed is, I don't know if we talked about this, but we were working with an ASCO agency.

So our own blog posts are also contributing to this, so that be.
This probably, like here, it says, yeah, but we should probably add, like, whatever we want to have in our narrative, like the, I think everything here, probably, instead of these things.
Mm-hmm.
Yeah, yeah.
I think, okay, just give me a minute.
I need to kick my cat out of my room.
I'm so sorry.
Yeah, we've seen this with other clients as well.
Like, it's kind of a, to change the narrative, like, almost like a multi-post approach where we kind of mention it in multiple different articles.
And then that'll start to kind of get the AI answers to come around that, especially if it's fresher and it's mentioned multiple times.
So we've done that just recently with
Yeah, yeah.

Okay, so I think a really aggressive way to target this and to kind of like just turn things around in terms of the messaging is to do a lot of like very bottom of the funnel articles that are kind of more comparison oriented.
So versus or alternatives articles.
And that would be kind of like, yeah, Dream Data versus HockeyStack.
Let's see.
Yeah, you have a blog that's Dream Data Alternatives, but you don't have one that's for like, specifically for that keyword.
So I think that's the, that's like the easier way, the easiest way to kind of like start showing up as like an enterprise solution.
And we make it really obvious.
would

Blogs that you are also supporting like enterprises and then we can adapt and update the artifacts that we're using for content generation and make it really even like more explicit that you are an enterprise platform so that that's also going to be helpful in addition to like you updating any old blog posts where you're showing up as like a small business solution.

They also put it here, there's like cross-posting, cross-posting, this is important, this is a good thing, does this impact search results because now here it's referencing this article, is that a good thing to do cross-posting?
Um, that honestly, short term, yes, long term, no, because it's seen as like Google likes original content.

So you should never like plagiarize and cross post to other websites like the exact same content because like right now it's working for them, but there could be like a Google core update or just like once Google understands like the crawlers understand that this type of content exactly appears the same on another website word for word, that blog post is going to go down in rankings and there are other blogs and other like other just other pages on their site could also go down in rankings.
I don't recommend doing that, like even though in the short term like it does give them more coverage, I think it hurts your SEO.
Yeah.
Okay.
And those are usually kind of the pay to play for the for backlinking as well.
Yeah, yeah, exactly.
So I think, I mean, you could, I mean, yeah, the backlinking, you know, they could be like repurposing.
repurposing the blog and like turning it into something else and then like just rewriting it slightly and then publishing it on a different website.
But that is like a pay to play thing for, I think the, we could just add a cluster to our content strategy that just targets a lot of these like bottom of the funnel keywords.
Try to find like, I'm sure there are gaps, so you do have some alternatives articles, but I do think that there are also gaps in terms of just like your competitors.
And then we could generate some of that content and get it up on your website.

The only thing is that with this type of content, like some companies want to have like a team or like someone on the product team review it just to make sure that like it's very highly, highly accurate.
And like it's like reflecting the product correctly.
So that's just one thing that.
I wanted to flag ahead of time if there's, like, any, if there are any people that would want to, like, review this content just for the product mentions.
Yeah, Amalia can review it.
I can review it too.
Amalia is our product market manager.
So she wasn't in the last calls.
She can review it.
Okay.
Okay.
That makes sense.
We also have your, okay.
Let me pull it up really quickly.
Yeah, and CaliberBind and StreamData, those guys are kind of the marketing intelligence attribution competitors.
I think we wanted to also get the account Intel competitors as well.
Yeah.
Yeah, so all of the competitors, basically across, like, all of the different tools, like, even if there's, like, a 50% overlap, have a five
We want to get in those conversations.
Yeah, but I want to fix this problem first because it's affecting our day-to-day prospects.
And they start to see this search results now, and they start to evaluate based on that.
Focus on this first, and then we can go abroad.
Yep.
Yeah, yeah, okay.
That makes sense.

I think we can get on that tomorrow and just, like, do some keyword research and then generate those assignments and then put them in Airtable, and then we can start prioritizing which ones we want to publish first.
Okay.
The other thing, too, is these topics that we have in ContentOS, a lot of them are not relevant to us.
I don't know if this is, like, the updated version after we talked last week, or if it's not updated yet.
But...
Yeah, we'll be updating that this week as well.
Yeah.
Okay.
I just want to make sure, as you know, we have the two-month pilot.
We are in the third week.
I just want to make sure we are at a stage where we can move forward and I can come into our finance team to move forward as well to the year's annual commitment.
That's why I'm trying to make sure that we are on the same page.
But if it's going to be this week, again, before these, I don't know if we can do it at the same time.
If it's at the same time, it's good for me too.
But I want to focus on these, like changing these things, these narrative things before the traffic focus pages.
Or if we can do it both at the same time, if it's not going to impact it because AI will help us, then I'm fine with that too.
Okay.
Okay.
Yeah, that makes sense.
I think just because I wasn't here.
Last week, I think I would just like to get your thoughts on the assignments that are already in the ContentOS and like some of them, like I know that you approved two.
So the ones that were approved versus the ones that weren't approved, I just wanted to get your thoughts on like what you think of the assignments in there.
Yeah, we went through the Notion documents last week and then we changed like our ICPs a little bit more and then changed the topics on Notion.
So like there are a lot of forecasting, lead qualification, forecasting and stuff like that that we don't do.
And then, yeah, there's like six or seven forecasting posts here, but we don't do forecasting as a platform.
Or like there is influencer marketing and then there was one more, affiliate marketing.
are not like, I mean, influencer marketing is a little bit more, but affiliate marketing is not a B2B use case.
So we don't get that as a use case that much from our product.
Cross-device tracking is something that we don't do, and it's like no one else does as well, it's very hard.
Those are reasons, mainly it's because we don't do, like we don't have these use cases, or it's our ICP doesn't care about it, that's why we didn't approve it.
I don't know if, I think Emilia went over this, I think she was waiting for Thursday, but if it's more helpful, we can just share them on Slack going forward as well, without waiting for the call.
Yeah, think, yeah, I think the interest of time, you can share it over Slack as well.

Okay, I think, yeah, let's, we'll get, we'll prioritize just the bottom of the funnel keywords, we'll create that new cluster, and then I'm sure that like most of those assignments in that cluster are going to be approved by you, so we can like start, we're already generating one.

Blog this week, and it's just to like align on the voice and tone, so we're not like losing time, so it's going to be ready for you by the end of the week, so you can take a look and see if like the writing is on point, and things like that, but in terms of like the bottom of the funnel keywords, we'll also get on that tomorrow, upload those assignments to the content OS, and then we can take it from there.
I think the only outstanding item is getting access to Google Console and search analytics, and then access for publishing.

Okay.
Yeah.
Because we typically do publishing for our clients.
Okay.
All right.

The artifacts have been updated.
So I think we should be good to go on that front.
I don't think there's anything else.

So you're using Profound to track your AI visibility, right?
Yeah.
Okay.
So we're using Scrunch, which is a Profound competitor.
And we have a dashboard set up for you that we set up, I think, two weeks ago.
Sorry, this is the wrong one.
I previewed it last week.
Okay.
So you saw it.
Yeah.
So that's where we're tracking your mentions.
And I think once we have access to your GA4 and GSC, we will create a Looker dashboard for you.
And the Looker dashboard.

Okay.
Cool.
I will share the, um, Google Search Console and, um, analytics right now.
Okay.
All right.
Perfect.
All right.

Okay.

For each competitor, is that how you want to do it or?
Yeah.

Yeah, we have it on Notion.
I can just write the Notions.
Okay.
Yeah, yeah, that's perfect.
Cool.

Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review HockeyStack's content strategy and address AI-driven narrative misalignment issues.](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [AI search results incorrectly portray HockeyStack as an SMB solution, contrary to their enterprise focus](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=375.0)
  - [Aggressive content strategy needed to correct narrative, including bottom-of-funnel comparison articles](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=603.0)
  - [Current ContentOS topics require updating to align with HockeyStack's actual offerings and ICP](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=944.0)
  - [Access to Google Search Console and analytics pending for comprehensive performance tracking](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=1170.0)

## Topics

### AI Narrative Misalignment

  - [Competitor (RSCAL) creating content portraying HockeyStack as SMB-focused](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=398.0)
  - [Google and ChatGPT results show HockeyStack lacking AI features (opposite of reality)](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=375.0)
  - [HockeyStack's own blog posts contributing to misalignment (e.g., "Dream Data for Enterprise")](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=509.0)
  - [Strategy: Create multiple posts mentioning correct narrative to influence AI answers](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=470.0)

### Content Strategy Correction

  - [Focus on bottom-of-funnel, comparison-oriented articles (e.g., "Dream Data vs HockeyStack")](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=625.0)
  - [Update content generation artifacts to explicitly highlight enterprise focus](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [Revise existing blog posts that may portray HockeyStack as a small business solution](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=603.0)
  - [Avoid cross-posting (duplicate content) as it can harm long-term SEO despite short-term benefits](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=712.0)

### ContentOS Topic Refinement

  - [Many current topics in ContentOS not relevant to HockeyStack (e.g., forecasting, lead qualification)](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=1049.0)
  - [Need to align with updated ICPs and actual product offerings](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=1039.0)
  - [Remove topics like influencer marketing, affiliate marketing, and cross-device tracking](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=1067.0)

### Performance Tracking

  - [HockeyStack using Profound for AI visibility tracking](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=1221.0)
  - [GrowthX set up Scrunch dashboard for mention tracking](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=1228.0)
  - [Pending access to GA4 and GSC for creating Looker dashboard](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=1170.0)
  - [Looker will show referral traffic from LLMs (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude)](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=1259.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Create new content cluster targeting bottom-of-funnel keywords](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=603.0)
  - [Generate and prioritize new content assignments in Airtable](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=1115.0)
  - [Share content approvals/feedback via Slack for efficiency](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=1102.0)
  - [HockeyStack to provide access to Google Search Console and analytics](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=1187.0)
  - [HockeyStack to share Notion doc with competitor differentiators table](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=1307.0)
  - [GrowthX to deliver first blog post by end of week for voice/tone alignment](https://fathom.video/share/41hN8_b6hdVaeWaghK1NN6xiiaxXyB2j?tab=summary&timestamp=1296.0)

action_items.description: Create new content cluster for bottom-of-funnel keywords, focus on competitor comparisons. Add to ContentOS.
Conduct keyword research for competitor comparison articles (vs/alternatives). Prioritize addressing enterprise narrative.
Generate content assignments for competitor comparisons based on keyword research. Upload to Airtable for prioritization.
Share Google Search Console and GA4 access with GrowthX team
Share Notion pages containing competitor differentiators table with GrowthX team
